summary: Full-stack regression for Subscription Overload Manager (Node.js/Express + React). 18/18 backend pytest cases pass and all critical UI flows (login, dashboard with 4 Recharts, subscription CRUD via modal, filters, profile update with budget=50, dark/light theme toggle with persistence, email-check toast, in-app notifications, budget-exceeded alert) work end-to-end. Verify-email flow validated via mongosh token lookup. Resend /api/email/test returns 200 with id (success).

issues: Recharts emits repeated console warnings 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on first paint. Charts still render correctly afterwards. Cosmetic only — consider wrapping ResponsiveContainer with explicit minWidth/minHeight or aspect prop to silence."

The success/welcome sonner toast appears at top-right and visually overlaps the theme-toggle button for ~5s. Buttons remain clickable but it's a minor UX papercut — consider repositioning or shortening the welcome toast.

action_items: (Optional) Silence Recharts width(-1) warnings by giving each <ResponsiveContainer> a fixed aspect or minHeight to avoid 0-px first-frame layout.
(Optional) Auto-dismiss the 'Welcome back' toast faster or move it so it does not overlap the theme-toggle button on the dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.js: register/login both setAuthCookie AND return token in body — good. Cookie SameSite/secure flags should be reviewed in middleware/auth.js for cross-site preview environments (Bearer fallback already in place via interceptor).
subscriptions.js DELETE returns 200 instead of 204; not a bug but inconsistent with REST conventions.

dashboard.js: 'monthly' chart simply repeats current totalMonthly for all 6 months and 'trend' is just a cumulative of that — there is no real per-month historical breakdown using lastPaymentDate. Acceptable for MVP but document this so users don't think it's per-month historical data.

subscriptions.js calcNextRenewal uses lastPaymentDate || renewalDate as base which is reasonable; supports only 'monthly' / 'yearly' (no weekly/quarterly) — fine if model BILLING_CYCLES enumerates only these.
Subscriptions.jsx empty state does NOT render data-testid='subscriptions-table' — by design (renders 'No subscriptions yet' instead). Tests should branch accordingly.
Frontend api.js correctly uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with credentials and Bearer interceptor — no hardcoded URLs found.
Mongo _id is exposed as _id (not transformed to id) in subscription/user responses; UI consumes _id directly — consistent across app, fine.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (login, dashboard charts/KPIs, sub CRUD, filters, profile, budget alert, theme toggle, email-check)

test_credentials: test@example.com / test1234 (seeded, working). Newly-registered qa+<uuid>@example.com accounts also work; verification token retrievable via mongosh test_database.
seed_data_creation: Tests create and clean up TEST_-prefixed subscriptions inside each test class. No persistent seed data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is Node.js/Express even though supervisor command shows uvicorn — server.py exec()s into Node. Use Bearer token (returned in body) rather than relying on cookies from cross-site preview URL. The /api/email/test endpoint sometimes returns 200 with Resend id, sometimes 502 if Resend rejects — both are accepted. mongosh CLI is available for direct DB inspection (e.g., verificationToken). Recharts log warnings about width(-1) are noise, not real bugs. Subscriptions table data-testid disappears in empty state — branch tests on items count.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA. Two failing assertions during initial run were caused by the shared requests.Session retaining the auth cookie after login, making /me and /email/test 'unauthorized' calls actually authenticated. Fixed by using a fresh requests.get/post (no cookies) for unauth assertions. Theme-toggle initially appeared not to toggle in headless run because the success toast overlapped the button at the same coordinates — re-tested after the toast cleared and toggle + persistence work correctly.
